message:
test(readme): cover Series binary arithmetic (fd90.228 / br-yugij)

Extends readme_element_wise_operations_compiles_and_runs to cover the
6 Series binary arithmetic operators at fp-frame:1391-1635 that
previously had no integration coverage:

- s.add(&other), s.sub(&other), s.mul(&other)
- s.div(&other), s.pow(&other), s.floordiv(&other)

Each takes another Series and aligns on index. Built a small 3-element
pair (a/b) with Float64 values for division-safe inputs.

Together with fd90.226/227 (DataFrame arithmetic) the binary
arithmetic surface is now fully exercised at both Series and
DataFrame levels.

Verified: 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-yu3w","title":"fd90.32: SqlConnection::table_comment for backend-agnostic table-comment introspection","description":"Add SqlConnection::table_comment(table, schema) -> Result<Option<String>, IoError> trait method for SQLAlchemy.Inspector.get_table_comment() parity. Default impl returns Ok(None) — SQLite has no native table-comment storage so it inherits this default. PostgreSQL impls override using pg_description lookups; MySQL uses information_schema.tables.table_comment; MSSQL uses sys.extended_properties. Free-fn sql_table_comment wrapper. Tests cover SQLite-None, default-None, multi-backend stub returning a comment, multi-backend stub returning None for unknown table.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-25T21:26:51.947523553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T21:29:28.091493876Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T21:29:28.087842555Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fd90","review","sql"]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-yugij","title":"fd90.228: Cover Series binary arithmetic (add/sub/mul/div/pow/floordiv)","description":"fd90.228: Cover Series binary arithmetic operators (add/sub/mul/div/pow/floordiv).

Series has 6 binary arithmetic methods at fp-frame:1391-1635 (add, sub, mul, div, floordiv, pow) that take another Series and align on index. None are integration-tested. The DataFrame counterparts (add_df/etc.) are now fully covered by fd90.226 + fd90.227.

Scope:
- Extend readme_element_wise_operations_compiles_and_runs (or add new) with all 6 Series binary arithmetic calls.

Built a small 3-element pair (a/b) with Float64 values for division-safe inputs. Now both DataFrame (fd90.226+227) and Series binary arithmetic surfaces are integration-tested. All 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-yujr6","title":"fd90.202: Add 7 README-documented error types to prelude (TypeError/ColumnError/etc.)","description":"fd90.202: Add all README-documented error types to the prelude.

The README's "Error Architecture" section (lines 829-853) documents 8 typed error enums and explicitly says "All error types are re-exported through the frankenpandas facade crate, so users can pattern-match without importing internal crates" — with a code example using them via `use frankenpandas::{IoError, FrameError, ExprError}`.

Today only JoinError is in the prelude. The other 7 (TypeError, ColumnError, IndexError, FrameError, ExprError, GroupByError, IoError) are exported at the top level only. User code following the README pattern of `use frankenpandas::prelude::*` then doing `match err { FrameError::… }` would not compile — the existing readme_apply_and_transform / readme_column_manipulation tests had to add `use frankenpandas::FrameError;` as a workaround.

Scope:
- Add TypeError, ColumnError, IndexError, FrameError, ExprError, GroupByError, IoError to the prelude module
- Add an integration test (or extend prelude_completeness_compile_guard) that uses each error type from the prelude
- Remove the workaround `use frankenpandas::FrameError;` lines from existing tests (now redundant)

Files: crates/frankenpandas/src/lib.rs + crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs.
Time estimate: 25-35min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T20:58:38.076743362Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T21:01:14.893770384Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T21:01:14.891164077Z","close_reason":"Added 7 error types (TypeError, ColumnError, IndexError, FrameError, ExprError, GroupByError, IoError) to the prelude module. JoinError was already there, so all 8 error types from the README's Error Architecture table (lines 829-853) are now usable via 'use frankenpandas::prelude::*'. Removed two  workaround imports from existing tests (now redundant). Added readme_error_architecture_compiles_and_runs as a compile-time guard against future regressions. All 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-yvkrb","title":"fd90.145: remove dead FrameError::DuplicateIndexUnsupported variant (followup to fd90.140 docs cleanup)","description":"fd90.74-144: follow-on to fd90.140. The FrameError::DuplicateIndexUnsupported variant declared at crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:113 is dead code — declared but never constructed anywhere in the workspace:

  grep -rnE 'DuplicateIndexUnsupported' crates/
  → only the declaration; zero call-site constructions

fd90.140 already removed it from the README's Error Architecture row. Now do the corresponding code cleanup. The enum is marked '#[non_exhaustive]' (line 108), so removing a variant is SemVer-safe — downstream code can't construct or exhaustively match the enum without acknowledging future additions/removals.

The error message ('duplicate index labels are unsupported in strict mode for MVP slice') reads like reserved-for-future-MVP language. If/when strict-mode duplicate handling lands, the variant can be re-added with proper construction sites — at that point the README listing will also update.

Fix: remove the variant + its #[error] attribute. cargo build -p fp-frame and cargo test -p fp-frame --lib should both pass with no changes since nothing references it.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T13:49:29.487955759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:51:20.033526080Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T13:51:20.030804620Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
